title: spec-deep report (machine-readable)
description: JSON form of a spec-deep audit report. Structured fields mirror the frontmatter; body sections are carried as markdown strings so the original report can be reconstructed or sliced.

commit.description: Commit hash the audit was run against.

artefact.description: Registry artefact verified against the commit, e.g. 'npm lilconfig@3.1.3'. Free text; may include a parenthetical diff note.

spec_version.description: spec-deep version this report was produced under.

model.description: Model name and version that produced the report.

boundaries.description: Trust boundaries named at the start of Phase 1.

boundaries_narrative.description: The prose 'Trust boundaries' section from the report body. Expands on the structured list with rationale.

sink_primitives.description: Per-language mapping from spec sink class to the concrete primitives grepped for. Corresponds to the 'X primitives mapped to spec sink classes' table.

inventory.description: Phase 1 sink inventory. Every sink in the codebase, before judgement.

inventory_notes.description: Prose accompanying the inventory: grep targets that returned nothing, artefact packaging notes, anything that is about the inventory as a whole rather than one sink.

findings.description: Sinks that survived all six steps. Empty array for a clean report.

ruled_out.description: Sinks that did not become findings, with the step that stopped each and why. Every inventory sink id should appear either here or in a finding's `sinks` list.

prior_art.description: Report-level prior-art search: git log greps, advisory database lookups, issue tracker review. Per-finding prior art lives on the finding.

reach.description: Report-level reach: dependent counts, download numbers, top consumers and how they call the library. Per-finding reach lives on the finding.

controls.description: What this actor supplies to the library.

source.description: Where the trust judgement is documented, or 'derived' if inferred.

primitive.description: The concrete language construct or sub-kind: 'require', 'JSON.parse', 'TOCTOU access-then-read', 'SQL', 'FFI', 'regex backtracking'. Free text. Optional when the class alone is specific enough.

sinks.description: One or more inventory sinks this ruling covers (entries are often grouped, e.g. S1/S2/S3 share a ruling).

reason.description: The body of the ruled-out entry: trace, what stopped it, any reproduction snippet. The full per-step prose, not a one-liner.

summary.description: One-line gist of why it was ruled out, for tabulation. Optional; derived from `reason` if absent.

reach_exposed.description: Number of examined dependents found exposed.

trace.description: Step 1: backwards trace from sink to library boundary.

boundary.description: Step 2: which trust boundary the input crosses and who controls it.

validation.description: Step 3: reproduction script and its output.

prior_art.description: Step 4: issue/PR/commit search specific to this finding.

reach.description: Step 5: dependent exposure specific to this finding.

rating.description: Step 6: severity justification and confidence statement.
